name: github_update_file_on_branch
description: Update a file on a specific branch in one atomic operation. Automatically handles SHA retrieval, content encoding, and branch targeting. This is the recommended way to update files as part of a branch workflow.

path.description: Path to the file in the repository

content.description: New file content (will be base64 encoded automatically)

message.description: Commit message for the file change

branch.description: Branch to update the file on. Function will automatically get the current SHA from this branch.

workflow: SINGLE STEP: Call this function with the target branch name. It will automatically: 1) Get the current file SHA from that branch, 2) Update the file with your content, 3) Commit to the specified branch. No manual SHA handling required!
internal_steps: 2. PUT /repos/{owner}/{repo}/contents/{path} with SHA and content to update file

examples.name: Update README on feature branch
examples.description: Update a file on a specific branch - SHA handled automatically

parameters.content:
# Updated README

This is the new content.
